body:
Hey Gloria,

Just a quick nudge - the monthly progress note for the survey steering group is due by end of day Thursday and I noticed you haven't filled in your section yet. No pressure, but the dean gets a bit twitchy when it's incomplete before the Friday morning faculty sync.

Let me know if you need the link again or if something came up this week. Happy to cover the field-logistics update for you in the meeting if you're slammed.

Karin
snippet:
Hey Gloria,

Just a quick nudge - the monthly progress note for the survey steer...
subject: Survey Progress Note - Reminder

body:
Karin, thank you for the heads-up! I completely lost track of the day - been buried in the harbour permit review for the NF-7 site since yesterday afternoon.

I just filled in my section now. Added a note about the Phase 3 dive window being pushed to early August, and flagged the sediment scour we found on the eastern trench. Can you give it a quick look before you submit? I want to make sure the wording on the scour doesn't sound more alarming than it needs to.

Also, coffee Thursday morning before the sync? I feel like we haven't caught up properly in weeks.

Gloria
snippet: Karin, thank you for the heads-up! I completely lost track of the day - been bur...
subject: Re: Survey Progress Note - Reminder

body:
Hey Gloria! Hope your Monday isn't too chaotic. I was thinking we're overdue for a proper lunch - it feels like we've been eating sad desk sandwiches at the institute for weeks now.

Are you free Wednesday or Thursday around noon? I've been wanting to try that new fish place down by the Speicherstadt. Apparently the matjes is worth the hype.

Let me know what works!
snippet: Hey Gloria! Hope your Monday isn't too chaotic. I was thinking we're overdue for...
subject: Lunch this week?

body:
Karin! Yes, absolutely - I was literally just thinking the same thing. Thursday works perfectly for me, Wednesday I have that awful 11:30 museum call that always runs over.

I walked past that place last week and it smelled incredible, so I'm very on board. Shall we say 12:15 so we're not fighting the first wave of the lunch crowd?

So looking forward to catching up properly!
snippet: Karin! Yes, absolutely - I was literally just thinking the same thing. Thursday ...
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body:
Thursday 12:15 it is! I'll book us a table just in case - I have a feeling it gets busy.

And yes, we have SO much to catch up on. I have thoughts about the coastal heritage mapping proposal I need your opinion on, but also completely non-work gossip that cannot wait any longer. Come hungry on both counts.

See you Thursday!
snippet: Thursday 12:15 it is! I'll book us a table just in case - I have a feeling it ge...
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body:
Hi Gloria,

I hope you're doing well! I've been heads-down on the terrestrial survey for the Husum hinterland project and I've finally got the stratigraphic interpretation to a point where I'd love a second set of eyes. You always catch things I miss, and honestly your read on the Birka comparanda saved us a lot of headaches down the line.

Would you have time this week or early next to do an informal review? I can send over the section drawings and the site narrative ahead of time so you can come prepared. It shouldn't take more than an hour or two.

Let me know what works for you!

Karin
snippet:
Hi Gloria,

I hope you're doing well! I've been heads-down on the terrestrial su...
subject: Interpretation Review Request - Husum Hinterland Survey

body:
Karin! Of course, I'd be happy to take a look. I've been curious about that survey ever since you mentioned the geophys anomalies back in February - the mix of medieval and possibly earlier features sounded like a fun puzzle.

Thursday afternoon works well for me, any time after 2pm. Or if that's too soon, I'm free most of Monday next week. Send over the drawings whenever you're ready and I'll review them before we meet so we can actually dig into the details rather than spending the whole time orienting me.

Looking forward to it!

Gloria
snippet: Karin! Of course, I'd be happy to take a look. I've been curious about that surv...
subject: Re: Interpretation Review Request - Husum Hinterland Survey

body:
Thursday at 3pm is perfect! I'll send a calendar invite now.

I'm attaching the section set (12 sheets) and a short site narrative - about 4 pages. The main things I'd love your input on are the phasing logic in trenches 2 and 3, and how to read the ditch alignment on the north edge. The county heritage office is a bit torn between calling it a medieval boundary or something earlier, and I want to have a stronger argument ready before the next steering meeting on the 24th.

Fair warning: the dating on the lower fills is still a bit hand-wavy - we're waiting on the radiocarbon samples. I flagged it in the narrative so you'll know where I'm less confident. We can skip over that part if you'd rather focus on the interpretation.

Karin
snippet:
Thursday at 3pm is perfect! I'll send a calendar invite now.

I'm attaching the ...
subject: Re: Interpretation Review Request - Husum Hinterland Survey

body:
Got the invite and the files - thank you! I've had a quick first look and I'm already intrigued. The section through trench 2 is really lovely, and I can see why the north ditch is giving you trouble. There's a lot riding on that call in terms of how the whole landscape phasing reads.

I'll do a proper read-through tonight and come Thursday with notes. Don't worry about the radiocarbon stuff, we can absolutely set that aside and focus on what matters before the 24th. If it would help, I can also draft a quick pros/cons summary on the boundary interpretation that you could hand directly to the heritage office - sometimes having it in writing makes those conversations easier.

See you Thursday!

Gloria
snippet: Got the invite and the files - thank you! I've had a quick first look and I'm al...
subject: Re: Interpretation Review Request - Husum Hinterland Survey

body:
Hey, just wanted to nail down a few things before the weekend at the coast. I was thinking we drive up to Husum instead of taking the train - we can stop in Schleswig for lunch and it's easier with all the dive kit. What do you think? Also, did you already sort the holiday flat in St. Peter-Ording or are we still looking?

Let me know soon so I can figure out whether to take the estate car or if yours is fine.
snippet: Hey, just wanted to nail down a few things before the weekend at the coast. I wa...
subject: Weekend logistics - North Frisian coast

body:
Driving up sounds good to me, I actually prefer it. And yes, I booked the holiday flat last week - it's confirmed for Saturday through Tuesday. I'll forward you the confirmation email.

The Schleswig lunch stop is a great idea. There's that fish smokehouse near the harbour we went to two years ago, I think it was just off the Schlossinsel. I'll try to find the name.

Can you pack the cool box? I'll handle snacks.
snippet: Driving up sounds good to me, I actually prefer it. And yes, I booked the holida...
subject: Re: Weekend logistics - North Frisian coast

body:
Perfect, I'll get the cool box ready Thursday night. And I think the smokehouse you're thinking of is Foerster - we should call ahead, it gets busy on weekends.

Also, my mother wants to know if we're free Sunday afternoon. Your parents are only about 45 minutes from Husum in Kiel and Sigrid said she'd love to do a quick lunch. Up to you, I told her it depended on our plans.
snippet: Perfect, I'll get the cool box ready Thursday night. And I think the smokehouse ...
subject: Re: Weekend logistics - North Frisian coast

body:
Yes, Foerster! I'll make a reservation for around 12:30 if that works with the drive time.

Sunday lunch with my parents in Kiel is fine with me, just let's make sure we're back at the coast by early evening. I want to do the dyke walk before dinner. Can you text my mother and confirm? Also find out if she wants us to bring anything.

I'm starting to pack the dive bags tonight. So excited.
snippet: Yes, Foerster! I'll make a reservation for around 12:30 if that works with the d...
subject: Re: Weekend logistics - North Frisian coast

body:
12:30 at Foerster works great. I texted Sigrid and she said Sunday around 1pm is perfect, and just bring ourselves, no need for anything.

I mapped the route - looks like about 2 hours with the Schleswig stop, so we should leave by 9am Saturday to get in with plenty of daylight and have time to settle in. I'll fill up the car Thursday.

See you at home tonight. This is going to be a good weekend.
snippet: 12:30 at Foerster works great. I texted Sigrid and she said Sunday around 1pm is...
subject: Re: Weekend logistics - North Frisian coast

body:
Hey, just got the renewal notice from the Provinzial for the household and contents insurance. It's due July 3rd and they're quoting €640 this year - up about €50 from last year. I think it's still reasonable given what we'd pay elsewhere, but wanted to check with you before I just let it auto-renew. Also, should we look at adding cover for my field and diving equipment? After Nils had that kit stolen from the van last summer I've been second-guessing whether the basic policy is enough.

Let me know what you think. I can call them this week if you want to shop around first.
snippet: Hey, just got the renewal notice from the Provinzial for the household and conte...
subject: Household insurance renewal

body:
The €50 increase is annoying but honestly not surprising. I'd say go ahead and renew rather than spend a week chasing quotes for a difference that probably won't be worth it.

On the equipment cover - yes, I think we should add it. After Nils's situation I've thought about that too. See if they can extend it to cover the dive and field kit and find out what the difference is. If it's under €80 more a year I'd just do it. Better to have it and not need it.

Go ahead and handle it, just let me know the final amount so I can note it in the budget.

Gloria
snippet: The €50 increase is annoying but honestly not surprising. I'd say go ahead and r...
subject: Re: Household insurance renewal

body:
Hey Gloria, hope your week is going okay. I've been drafting the data-handover plan for the NF-7 sonar archive and wanted to run it by you before I send it to the steering group.

Current thinking is we cut over to the new processed-mosaic dataset on Saturday evening, give ourselves an afternoon to verify the georeferencing. We'll keep the old raw-line exports available read-only for 72 hours as a fallback, then archive them to the institute server Tuesday morning. The reprocessed bathymetry has been checked against the control points for two weeks and I'm feeling pretty good about it.

Main risk I see is the older field plans - some still reference the deprecated grid origin from the Phase 2 survey. Can you check with your students how many of the dive logs still use the old coordinate frame? That's where we'd have a mismatch.
snippet: Hey Gloria, hope your week is going okay. I've been drafting the data-handover p...
subject: NF-7 sonar archive handover - your thoughts?

body:
Karin, this looks really solid. Saturday afternoon works well - that's the quietest stretch for server access, so the re-georeferencing should go smoothly.

I checked the dive logs and it's not bad: about 2.3% still reference the old Phase 2 grid origin. Most of those are early test dives from before we standardised the frame, so they're largely superseded anyway. I'd say the risk is manageable, but we should probably send a note to the students Thursday reminding them to use the new origin going forward, just to cover ourselves.

One thing I'd add to the plan: can we make sure the backup-verification is explicitly assigned this weekend? Last archive handover I had to chase down who had confirmed the checksums for 20 minutes and it was not fun. Otherwise I think you're good to send this to the group.
snippet: Karin, this looks really solid. Saturday afternoon works well - that's the quiet...
subject: Re: NF-7 sonar archive handover - your thoughts?

body:
2.3% is way better than I was bracing for, honestly. Good call on the note to the students - I'll loop them in today to get that sent for Thursday.

I've already added an explicit verification-assignment section to the doc (sorry, should have had that from the start). You and I are listed as the checksum sign-off, which I hope is okay - figured since we both know this dataset cold, better us than someone reading the metadata notes at the last minute.

I'll send the finalized plan to the steering group this afternoon. If they approve by EOD Wednesday, we're a go for Saturday. Fingers crossed. And yes, after this is done we are absolutely getting dinner somewhere nice - I'm thinking that place on Schillerstraße you mentioned. Deal?
snippet: 2.3% is way better than I was bracing for, honestly. Good call on the note to th...
subject: Re: NF-7 sonar archive handover - your thoughts?

body:
Gloria,

Confirming the diver-day subcontract numbers for the Phase 3 close-out so your ledger lines up. For the early part of the season (weeks 12-18, up to end of January) I invoiced at my old rate of EUR 465.00 per diver-day. From 1 February onward the revised rate of EUR 520.00 per diver-day applies - that's the mid-season increase we agreed after the tank-handling and extra safety-cover load went up on the NF-7 grid.

So it's 8 days at 465 for the first block and 6 days at 520 for the second block. Both bills are in the university's Xero under my name (BILL-3010 early, BILL-3011 mid-season) if you need the paper trail for the DFG reviewer.

Nils
snippet:
Gloria,

Confirming the diver-day subcontract numbers for the Phase 3 close-out s...
subject: Diver-day subcontract rate — mid-season change confirmation

body:
Thanks Nils - that matches what I had in my head. Just to write it down so I can defend it line by line to the liaison:

- Weeks 12-18: 8 diver-days @ EUR 465.00 = EUR 3,720.00 net (BILL-3010, paid)
- Weeks 20-24: 6 diver-days @ EUR 520.00 = EUR 3,120.00 net (BILL-3011, authorised)
- Changeover effective 1 February 2026

I'll reconcile that against the department ledger and the QuickBooks day-rate items before the Phase 3 report goes in. Shout if any of those day counts look off to you.

— Gloria
snippet: Thanks Nils - that matches what I had in my head. Just to write it down so I can ...
subject: Re: Diver-day subcontract rate — mid-season change confirmation
